name: Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular from Matarrosa del Sil by the Cerro de la Laguna
name_original: Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular desde Matarrosa del Sil por el Cerro de la Laguna
region_intro: Jogging around Toreno offers a variety of routes through a landscape characterized by hills, lagoons, and elevated viewpoints. The region features varied terrain, including paths that ascend to "cerros" (hills) and "altos" (high points), providing diverse running experiences. Trails often incorporate natural features such as the Laguna de San Pedro Mallo and offer panoramic views from locations like La Gualta. Best jogging routes around Toreno The most popular jogging route is Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular from Matarrosa del Sil by the Cerro de la Laguna, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This circular route explores the area around a lagoon and a prominent hill. Another top favourite among local runners is Alto de Fasquedo Llamazal loop from Santa Cruz del Sil, a difficult 22.1 miles (35.6 km) path. This extensive loop offers a challenging run through elevated terrain. Local runners also love the Collada del Cancerbeiro loop from Santa Cruz del Sil, a 9.7 miles (15.6 km) trail leading through a mountain pass, often completed in about 2 hours 11 minutes. Jogging around Toreno is defined by hills, lagoons, and scenic viewpoints, with routes offering options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Toreno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 120 runners have used komoot to explore Toreno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Toreno?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;16&lt;/strong&gt; running routes around Toreno listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: What is the overall difficulty level of running trails in Toreno?
A: The running trails in Toreno are primarily categorized as moderate or difficult. There are &lt;strong&gt;8 moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes and &lt;strong&gt;8 difficult&lt;/strong&gt; routes, so runners should be prepared for challenging terrain and elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes in Toreno?
A: Yes, Toreno offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056012446/laguna-de-san-pedro-mallo-running-circular-from-matarrosa-del-sil-by-the-cerro-de-la-laguna" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de San Pedro Mallo – running circular from Matarrosa del Sil by the Cerro de la Laguna&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option that explores a lagoon and a prominent hill. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collada del Cancerbeiro loop from Santa Cruz del Sil&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through a mountain pass.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Toreno?
A: Jogging around Toreno is characterized by a diverse landscape of hills, lagoons, and elevated viewpoints. Routes often ascend to 'cerros' (hills) and 'altos' (high points), providing varied terrain and scenic vistas.

Q: Are there running routes with good viewpoints in Toreno?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Toreno offer panoramic views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24568513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Gualta Viewpoint loop from Noceda&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase stunning vistas. Additionally, you can find excellent views of the Bárcena Reservoir from highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5470306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Bárcena Reservoir and the Compostilla II power plant&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls in Toreno?
A: Yes, you can find natural attractions like waterfalls near Toreno's running routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151921" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gualta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the area that runners might encounter or visit nearby.

Q: What do other runners think about the routes in Toreno?
A: The running routes in Toreno are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from over 1 review. More than 120 runners have explored these varied terrains, often praising the scenic beauty and challenging nature of the trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Toreno?
A: While there are no routes specifically classified as 'easy' in Toreno, the &lt;strong&gt;8 moderate&lt;/strong&gt; routes offer less extreme challenges than the difficult ones. A good option to consider is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 8.6 km path with a more manageable elevation gain.

Q: Are there any running routes suitable for families in Toreno?
A: Given that most routes are moderate to difficult, families with younger children or those seeking very gentle paths might find options limited. However, families with older, more active children could enjoy the moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31892745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Toreno&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a good balance of distance and elevation.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Toreno?
A: Toreno's diverse landscape can be enjoyed throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, which are ideal for running. Summer can be warm, especially at midday, while winter might bring cooler conditions, but the trails remain accessible for those prepared for the weather.

Q: Are there any less crowded running spots in Toreno?
A: While specific crowd levels vary, exploring routes during off-peak hours (early mornings or late afternoons) or choosing less-trafficked trails can help you find quieter running experiences. The more extensive and challenging routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24569782" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Fasquedo Llamazal loop from Santa Cruz del Sil&lt;/a&gt;, might naturally be less crowded due to their difficulty and length.

Q: Are there any natural monuments or unique features to explore along the running routes?
A: Yes, Toreno's surroundings are rich in natural features. Beyond the Gualta Waterfall, you can discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5556546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zofreral de Arriba&lt;/a&gt; forest or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151920" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sulphur Spring&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de la Rebeza&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse points of interest.
tips: Near the town of Villamartín del Sil, we find this beautiful dirt road. Without a doubt, a beautiful picture surrounded by nature, quite possibly walking towards the San Pedro Mallo Lagoon.

Located in an environment of pure nature and tranquility, the small lagoon of San Pedro Mallo is an ideal place to relax and follow a very pleasant and relaxed itinerary. Very close to here is the recreational area with everything you need to have a picnic if you prefer.
